column1: # DSGV Hub Firmware — Partition Table (4 MB flash)
# Layout: dual-bank OTA + small user NVS storage
# │ otadata     │   8 KB   │ Bootloader OTA state (which bank is active)     │
# │ phy_init    │   4 KB   │ RF / PHY calibration data                       │
# │ ota_1       │  1.8 MB  │ OTA update slot (bootloader rolls back on crash)│
# │ user_nvs    │ 256 KB   │ Reserved NVS — extended device config / future  │
#   The bootloader reads otadata to decide which slot (ota_0 or ota_1) is
#   the active firmware. During an OTA update
column2: new firmware is written to the

column1: #   inactive slot. If the new firmware crashes before calling

column2: the bootloader rolls back to the

column1: #   previous slot automatically — the device can never be permanently bricked
# Hard build limit enforced by scripts/check_binary_size.py.
#   Separate NVS partition kept for future extended configuration (custom

column3: etc.). A dedicated partition means a factory reset

column1: #   of user_nvs does not touch Wi-Fi creds or device identity in the main nvs.
# For 8 MB flash (recommended for new hardware spins): use partitions_8mb.csv
